item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Did the name of this skip remind anyone else of that horror movie Krampus?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As people have pointed out, the dialogue is a bit cheesy… but i upvoted for the end. Wholesome scary scps are really hard to do.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I generally like this, but it falls apart during the description of the effects.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: remember kids, this means that any of these creatures can be real with the power of evil nightmare snow globe, and that just isn't right.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The book thing felt rally cliché (correct me if I'm wrong, but isn't it so that you're incapable of reading anything in a dream and all text just apperas as a mass of jumbled letters?), but I liked the rest. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Remove the negative emotions and thoughts from your mind, with your friends at The Oneiroi Collective. Do not use more than three times per 30 days. In case of emergency, DO NOT BREAK THE GLASS."

This reads more like a Factory thing than an Oneiroi Collective thing. I mean both this particular part, and the object itself. I thought the affects on the building were a bit too on the nose, and a bit too closely and obviously related to the farmer's nightmares about being bullied. It strikes me as narmy, almost cartoonish. I'm sorry, this is just really, thoroughly didn't do it for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I want to hug J. Not sure why.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: REM sleep, where dreams occur, happens several times during a full night's sleep. Might want to reduce the duration of the Alpha event and add something about selection of dream based on subconscious reaction or brainwave activity.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Others have already pointed out the same issues with minor errors in the article that I would cite. I'm fine with the tone though, personally; I didn't get any weird feelings from it.
Has good clarity, isn't too long/short, holds attention to the end.
All in all, I say that although the wordage issues do need fixed, it's not enough to prevent me from just going ahead and upvoting it now. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvoted for the interview, being a decent dream-related skip, and continuing from 1498, which I did not expect to ever have a continuation.

That said, there are numerous spots where the writing needs some brushing up. Though I don't consider the interview one of them; it occurred to me that the Doctor isn't talking to a little boy, but something else, so why wouldn't it talk like that, really?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the concept, and I like the beggining. But, as stated above, the dialogue feels robotic and awkward, and I feel like the end took a direction that doesn't really fit the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the story, small error here tho:

Doctor ██████: What else do know you know about this place?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As said by the posters above me, this is a very interesting SCP but with a slightly cheesy story tackled on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm kind of split on this. It's a good article and all but I think the fact that you took the whole 'physical manifestation of a nightmare' and went with a feelsy article left me feeling just a tad disappointed at the end. +1 nonetheless, can't always have everything my way I suppose.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As Bryx said the dialogue is stilted and not very realistic-sounding, but I quite like the rest of the piece. Reminds me a lot of something out of Psychonauts, in a very good way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Definitely good enough for an upvote, in my opinion, but the dialogue feels a bit cheesy.
